description: Strong junior evaluated against University of Florida (KB acceptance ~24.2%, VERY_SELECTIVE per fit_computation.py's tier bucketing — 15-25% band). No selectivity floor or ceiling applies in this band, so the LLM has full latitude to land any of the 4 categories. The Phase 1 invariants verify the response is structurally correct without pinning a specific category.
business_rationale: VERY_SELECTIVE schools are a real-world challenge for the algorithm: no hard floor or ceiling forces a specific category, so the LLM's judgement does the work. Synthetic monitoring of this band catches drift in the LLM's reasoning that the floor + ceiling rules in PRs #76/#78 wouldn't see — e.g., the model suddenly classifying a 24% school as SAFETY because it weighs profile strength too heavily.
tests: VERY_SELECTIVE band (15-25% acceptance): no floor + no ceiling, so any of {SAFETY, TARGET, REACH, SUPER_REACH} is technically valid; invariants verify structure
match_percentage falls in whichever band the chosen category declares
All four factors {Academic, Holistic, Major Fit, Selectivity} present with bounded scores
Selectivity factor in {-5, 0} band (per the rubric's 15-25% adjustment)
All 8 advisory blocks present and non-empty
